PARAM.description: time must be a valid double value
PARAM.assertion: assert args[0] instanceof java.lang.Double && (args[0] >= 0 || args[0] > getFinalTime()) && (args[0] <= getInitialTime());
PARAM.name: time
PARAM.content:
<strong>This method should </strong>not be called before the integration is over because some internal variables are set only once the last step has been handled.</p>
<p>Setting the time outside of the integration interval is now allowed, but should be used with care since the accuracy of the interpolator will probably be very poor far from this interval.</p>
<p>Note that each time this method is called, the internal arrays returned in {@link #getInterpolatedState()}, {@link #getInterpolatedDerivatives()} and {@link #getInterpolatedSecondaryState(int)}</em> will be overwritten.</p>
<p>So if their content must be preserved across several calls, user must copy them.</p>
